data.title: Bank OZK GAAP EPS of $1.59 misses by $0.10, revenue of $449.93M beats by $3.07M
data.description:
• Bank OZK press release (NASDAQ:OZK): Q3 GAAP EPS of $1.59 misses by $0.10.
  • Revenue of $449.93M (+6.4% Y/Y) beats by $3.07M.
  • Shares -3.02% AH.

data.title: TeraWulf prices $3.2B offering of senior secured notes
data.description:
• TeraWulf (NASDAQ:WULF) on Thursday said its unit WULF Compute LLC priced a $3.2 billion offering of 7.75% senior secured notes due 2030 at par.

  • The private placement, expected to close on Oct. 23, will be sold to qualified institutional buyers under Rule 144A.

  • The company said proceeds will fund part of a data center expansion at its Lake Mariner campus in Barker, New York.
  • The notes will be guaranteed by several subsidiaries and secured by first-priority liens on substantially all assets of WULF Compute and its guarantors.
  • WULF +0.54% after hours to $13.93.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Newsmax announces crypto plan for asset reserve
data.description:
• Newsmax (NYSE:NMAX) said on Thursday that its board authorized a plan to purchase up to $5 million worth of Bitcoin and Trump Coin over the next 12 months, depending on market conditions.
  • Newsmax said it plans to make the first tranche of purchases soon and would be the first NYSE-listed company to hold Trump Coin.
  • Future acquisitions will depend on market conditions and operational needs.
  • NMAX +2.75% after hours to $11.17.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Praxis Precision Medicines announces proposed public offering
data.description:
• Praxis Precision Medicines (NASDAQ:PRAX) said on Thursday it plans to offer shares of its common stock and prefunded warrants in a public offering, with all securities to be sold by the company.
  • The size and pricing of the offering were not disclosed.
  • The company granted underwriters a 30-day option to buy up to 15% additional shares.
  • PRAX -2.92% after hours to $157.78.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Wall Street slides on trade war concerns, bank loans reports, ongoing U.S. shutdown
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages closed lower on Thursday amid increased volatility due to the ongoing U.S.-China trade conflict, and bad loans reports in banks, while investors continued to assess earnings reports and the ongoing U.S. government shutdown.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) closed -0.6%, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) finished -0.5%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) ended -0.7%. 

"Today was a great example of just how unfavorable the risk/reward for the market has become. Trading at 23.0x forward earnings, the market knows that any negative headlines could cause a sell-off due to the high dependence on elevated future growth,” Seeking Alpha analyst Leo Nelissen said.

Bank stocks took a dive after Zions Bancorporation (ZION) -13.2% and Western Alliance (WAL) -11.1% disclosed irregularities related to loans.

“While I do not believe today’s banking news is the start of any systematic weakness, we need to get used to elevated volatility on days when the focus isn’t on AI or other favorable developments," Nelissen said.

On earnings, companies such as Charles Schwab (SCHW) and U.S. Bancorp (USB) saw shares initially rise after earnings beat and earnings growth, but both closed lower at -1% and -1.8%, respectively.

In other tech news, Salesforce (CRM) +3.6% and Google (GOOGL) (GOOG) announced an expansion of their existing partnership on Thursday to bring Gemini AI models to the former Agentforce 360 Platform.

Over in the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 5 basis points to 3.98% -- its lowest level since April -- while the 2-year yield (US2Y) was 8 basis point lower at 3.43%.

The U.S. government shutdown is now entering its third week, as a GOP funding bill fails for the 10th time. Prediction market Kalshi now sees the government shutdown lasting more than 39 days.

Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said that China's lead trade negotiator Li Chenggang acted "unhinged" in Washington and warned that China would retaliate if the U.S. imposed port fees on its ships.

Bessent had earlier said that President Donald Trump "is a go" for a meeting with Xi Jinping and that he was positive about the ongoing discussions with China. 

In economic news, the NAHB/Wells Fargo Housing Market Index rose to 37 in October from 32 a month before, vs. 33 consensus.

U.S. stocks ended mixed after a choppy session on Wednesday, with the S&P 500 (SP500) and the Nasdaq (COMP:IND) gaining ground as upbeat earnings reports eclipsed U.S.-China trade tension worries.

data.title: Triumph Financial targets 20% annual transportation revenue growth while holding expenses flat through restructuring
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Triumph Financial (TFIN) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO Aaron Graft emphasized the evolution of Triumph Financial's business model, with "a focus on revenue growth continues to be sure, but also demonstrating a commitment to operating margin expansion." Graft highlighted progress through restructuring efforts designed to reduce the expense run rate and support revenue growth, stating the company "intend[s] to deliver" on 20% annual growth in transportation revenue and margin expansion through efficiency improvements.
  • Graft reported that the recently announced restructuring will "cut 5% of our expense base with the majority of those savings commencing in the fourth quarter." He explained that this restructuring also "organizes our go-to-market strategy around our customer verticals, brokers, carriers, shippers and factors."
  • Addressing a specific credit exposure, Graft stated, "At present, we believe we remain adequately secured in that credit. We remind investors that this is a highly fluid and evolving situation subject to ongoing legal proceedings."
  • CFO William Voss stated, "if you look at the way that we kind of framed our fourth quarter at 96.5% and I think Aaron mentioned during the first -- during his opening comments that we're looking to be right at about that level a quarter or a year from now."

Outlook

  • Graft maintained the target of 20% annual transportation revenue growth, reiterating, "We have called for 20% annual growth in transportation revenue, and we intend to deliver."
  • The company expects expenses to be "flat at this time next year" regardless of market conditions, with Graft specifying, "we expect revenue to go up and expenses to be flat at this time next year."
  • CFO Voss projected Q4 2025 expenses of $96.5 million, with a similar expense run rate for Q4 2026, and noted, "throughout the course of 2026, we're going to have to find more ways to be efficient."

Financial Results

  • In the payments segment, management noted that "if you were to just look at payments and you were not to include the investments we're making in LoadPay, the EBITDA margin in that payments segment would be almost 30%."
  • LoadPay, described as a "start-up piece of our payments business," reportedly doubled over last quarter. Graft stated, "a linked and funded LoadPay account is $750 of revenue" annually for seasoned accounts, with some accounts trending higher due to debit card usage.
  • Factoring revenue for the quarter was reported at "roughly $40 million." Graft highlighted a shift from limiting growth to prioritizing it, asserting, "this will be the first full year in many years where growth in factoring has been a very high priority for us."

Q&A

  • Matt Olney, Stephens Inc., asked about the Intelligence segment's product integration timeline. Dawn Salvucci-Favier responded, "The fully integrated product is actually in market right now as we speak."
  • Olney also inquired about factoring growth targets; Tim Valdez indicated, "Our target for growth is 20%... we're going to market right now with the most robust playbook that we ever have had."
  • Timothy Switzer, KBW, questioned the onboarding status of major clients for TPay. President Todd Ritterbusch said, "all of their payments volume is onboarded at this point," but added that revenue ramp is ongoing.
  • Joseph Yanchunis, Raymond James, probed the $50 million transportation revenue growth outlook and its components. Graft detailed contributions from factoring, payments, LoadPay, and intelligence, stating, "my expectation is that factoring is going to grow 20%, and we have a plan to go do that."
  • Gary Tenner, D.A. Davidson, requested clarity on expense management for 2026. Voss responded, "we are looking to continue to find ways to get more efficient across our entire platform throughout the year."
  • Analysts also raised questions on contract terms, competitive landscape, and progress on nonperforming loan recoveries, with management generally providing detailed, but sometimes guarded, responses.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed for specifics on ramping revenues, product integration, and expense control, signaling a slightly positive but cautious tone and seeking validation on growth and margin targets.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A. Graft repeatedly underscored conviction in the company's strategy, with statements such as "we have invested in our brand to be people who deliver value to our customers, and we're ready to go."
  • Compared to last quarter, management's tone shifted from discussing "noise" and distractions to emphasizing operational discipline and the tangible impact of restructuring.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The Q3 call placed greater emphasis on restructuring to drive margin expansion and holding expenses flat, compared to the Q2 call, which referenced "noise" from a major settlement and integration of acquisitions.
  • Management's confidence in targeting 20% transportation revenue growth was consistent, but this quarter included more specific commentary on cost control and expense run rates.
  • Analyst focus in Q3 shifted toward the ramp-up of integrated products, monetization timelines, and expense discipline, while Q2 questions were more focused on integration and competitive positioning.
  • Management responses this quarter were more explicit about realignment and efficiency, and there was increased discussion of leveraging technology and restructuring for operational leverage.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management flagged continued legal uncertainty regarding the Tricolor credit, calling it "a highly fluid and evolving situation subject to ongoing legal proceedings."
  • The potential impact of regulatory enforcement on the trucking market was discussed, with Graft noting that such enforcement "is going to cause more distortions in the for-hire market and the smaller end of that market."
  • Analysts expressed concern over the timing of revenue realization from new partnerships and the stickiness of LoadPay accounts. Management stated retention is high and ramping is underway.
  • Expense management and the effectiveness of the restructuring were noted as ongoing challenges, with management pledging further efficiency gains.

Final Takeaway

Triumph Financial's Q3 2025 call underscored a commitment to delivering 20% annual transportation revenue growth while holding expenses flat through restructuring and efficiency initiatives. The company highlighted operational progress in integrating technology and realigning go-to-market strategies, with management expressing confidence in margin expansion and future market share gains despite ongoing legal and market headwinds.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Gitlab jumps on report of takeover interest from Datadog
data.description:
Gitlab (NASDAQ:GTLB) quickly rose 11% amid renewed takeover speculation. 

Datadog (NASDAQ:DDOG) is reportedly exploring a renewed possible takeover of Gitlab for more than $60 a share, according to a StreetInsider item on Thursday. Shares of Datadog fell 5.4%.

The latest update comes after Betaville last week said that Gitlab (NASDAQ:GTLB) is speculated to have received  renewed takeover interest, though it didn't identify the potential buyer.

The latest speculation comes after a report in July 2024 that GitLab (NASDAQ:GTLB) was exploring a sale after the San Francisco-based company attracted acquisition interest. GitLab, which has investors including Google parent Alphabet, saw interest from peers, including cloud monitoring firm Datadog (NASDAQ:DDOG).

data.title: Vistra, PG&E top picks at TD Cowen in initiating coverage of U.S. power and utility sector
data.description:
TD Cowen tapped Vistra (NYSE:VST) and PG&E (NYSE:PCG) as its Top Picks in the U.S. power and utility sector, which it initiated with a "positive bias" in anticipation of a strong need for robust infrastructure in the face of accelerated demand for electricity from data centers, as well as the need to upgrade aging utility assets, meaning that power prices will remain elevated in key deregulated markets for the foreseeable future.

TD Cowen cited a straightforward case for Vistra (NYSE:VST), whose generation assets benefit from locational advantages, particularly around the fast-growing Dallas market, and the bank expects EBITDA to grow by an average 8.4% annually through 2030, driven by tightening power supply/demand, leading to higher prices for both energy and capacity.

Vistra (VST) is expected to benefit from structural tailwinds in the power space as electricity demand continues to grow faster than new generation can come online, and TD Cowen also finds the company's ERCOT footprint compelling vs. peers given the market dynamics in Texas outpacing the rest of the country as well as lower regulatory and political risk in future years.

California's chaos has created an attractive entry point, according to TD Cowen, with PG&E (NYSE:PCG), the state's largest utility, as its other Top Pick - although Buy-rated Edison International (EIX) is not far behind - viewing the recent passage of AB 254 as a step in the right direction, as the legislation further improves financial mitigation for large wildfires while delaying when utilities need to commit more capital; to the extent PG&E can continue to harden the grid against future wildfires, the potential to contribute more capital diminishes, the analysts said.

Also initiated with Buy ratings: American Electric Power (AEP), Duke Energy (DUK), NextEra Energy (NEE), Public Service Enterprise Group (PEG), Southern (SO), Xcel Energy (XEL).

Started at Neutral: American Water Works (AWK), Eversource Energy (ES), MDU Resources (MDU), Pinnacle West (PNW), WEC Energy (WEC).

ETF: (XLU)

data.title: Jefferies, Zions, Western Alliance sink, leading regional bank stocks down on credit risk concern
data.description:
At 3:53 PM ET updates with addition of Jefferies to stocks that are declining and updates stock price movement.

Stocks of investment bank Jefferies Financial (JEF) and regional banks Zions Bancorporation (NASDAQ:ZION), and Western Alliance tanked on Thursday as investors worried about the quality of loans and investments made by the firms. That's led to weakness across regional banks. The KBW Nasdaq Regional Banking Index (KRX) sank 6.8% in late Thursday trading, its biggest drop since April, following the disclosures by two banks of irregularities related to some loans. 

Jefferies (JEF) stock slid 10%, continuing a decline started when it revealed its exposure to bankrupt auto-parts maker First Brands last week.

After Wednesday's close, Zions Bancorporation (NASDAQ:ZION) said it found "apparent misrepresentations and contractual defaults" by the borrowers and supporting collateral when it reviewed two related loans made by its California Bank & Trust division. Zions took a provision for the full $60M outstanding under the loans.

On Thursday, Western Alliance Bancorporation (NYSE:WAL) said it sued a borrower, alleging fraud in failing to provide collateral loans in first position, among other claims.

Zions (NASDAQ:ZION) stock plunged 13% and Western Alliance (NYSE:WAL) tumbled 12% in Thursday trading.

That apparently has investors wary of other regional banks. Among the biggest decliners in the sector: Flagstar Financial (NYSE:FLG) -7.3%, Banc of California (NYSE:BANC) -8.3%, Webster Financial (NYSE:WBS) -7.7%, Metropolitan Bank Holding (NYSE:MCB) -8.7%, Axos Financial (NYSE:AX) -8.5%, and Bank OZK (NASDAQ:OZK) -6/0%.
